Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Dataframe 1.0.0.0 39 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 204 upvotes, 43 comments
  3. Can you get root with only a cigarette lighter? (2024) 97 upvotes, 18 comments
  4. Ordered dithering with arbitrary or irregular colour palettes (2023) 54 upvotes, 8 comments
  5. The way CTRL-C in Postgres CLI cancels queries is incredibly hack-y 83 upvotes, 16 comments
  6. POSSE – Publish on your Own Site, Syndicate Elsewhere 183 upvotes, 45 comments
  7. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 418 upvotes, 120 comments
  8. GrapheneOS will remain usable by anyone without requiring personal information 450 upvotes, 137 comments
  9. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 205 upvotes, 60 comments
  10. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 363 upvotes, 116 comments
  11. Five years of running a systems reading group at Microsoft 175 upvotes, 51 comments
  12. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 483 upvotes, 164 comments
  13. General Motors Is Assisting with the Restoration of a Rare EV1 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  14. GitHub appears to be struggling with measly three nines availability 75 upvotes, 24 comments
  15. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 662 upvotes, 313 comments
  16. The future of version control 553 upvotes, 307 comments
  17. Migrating to the EU 230 upvotes, 142 comments
  18. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 225 upvotes, 139 comments
  19. Attractive students no longer receive better results as classes moved online 40 upvotes, 18 comments
  20. Why I love NixOS 340 upvotes, 244 comments
  21. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 434 upvotes, 326 comments
  22. Jazz CRJ9 at New York on Mar 22nd 2026, collision with fire truck on runway 16 upvotes, 6 comments
  23. Tin Can, a 'landline' for kids 174 upvotes, 129 comments
  24. Walmart: ChatGPT checkout converted 3x worse than website 136 upvotes, 98 comments
  25. Show HN: The King Wen Permutation: [52, 10, 2] 28 upvotes, 14 comments
  26. Bombadil: Property-based testing for web UIs by Antithesis 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Trump Postpones Iran Energy Strikes for Five Days, Citing "Productive" Talks 12 upvotes, 6 comments
  28. You are not your job 226 upvotes, 256 comments
  29. What young workers are doing to AI-proof themselves 155 upvotes, 252 comments
  30. GoGoGrandparent (YC S16) is hiring Back end Engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments
